encounter.reason: Post-exacerbation follow-up discussion and biologic therapy consideration following severe asthma exacerbation and ED visit
encounter.location: UNC Medical Center, 101 Manning Drive, Chapel Hill, NC 27514

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: fluticasone/salmeterol (Advair HFA 115/21 mcg) inhaler
medications.sig: 1 puff inhaled twice daily via spacer

medications.name: albuterol (ProAir HFA 90 mcg) inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled PRN for wheezing/shortness of breath via spacer

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen Jr 0.15 mg)
medications.sig: two-pack, carried at all times, PRN for anaphylaxis
medications.reason: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

document_references.description: Documents Dr. Krishnamurthy's telephone follow-up with Priya Patel (mother) regarding Aisha's post-exacerbation status. Reviews the January 2025 WakeMed ED visit and Dr. Harlow's step-up to Advair HFA 115/21. Mother reports clinical stabilization with decreased rescue inhaler use. Discusses future omalizumab (Xolair) consideration when Aisha reaches age 6 (March 2026) and defers detailed peanut oral immunotherapy discussion to next in-person visit. Plan continues current asthma and allergy regimen with in-person follow-up scheduled for late summer/early fall 2025.
